reviews.title: Had a mortgage prodcuct transfer, I was offered bett...
reviews.text: Had a mortgage prodcuct transfer, I was offered better rates with Newcastle as my current provider, that any other provider could offer

reviews.title: Easy and simple
reviews.text: No issues, went through a third party but will probably go to NCBS direct next time as so easy.

reviews.title: Good rate and quick product transfer
reviews.text: I stayed with Newcastle Building Society after 2 years into my mortgage. They offered very good rate and the process was really simple to switch.

reviews.title: Wasting people's time
reviews.text: After offering us £20,000 more than we wanted on a mortgage principal certificate we decided to go with them both of us have good jobs and an excellent credit rating without a reason decided to decline the mortgage why offer a mortgage in principle if you take it away there slogan is to look after the people of the North East what a load of rubbish

reviews.title: Great rates and service
reviews.text: Everything has been simple and efficient with our mortgage application. The service has been brilliant and the rate is very competitive. I don't ever want to move my mortgage.

reviews.title: Mortgage application
reviews.text: Newcastle Building Society provided exactly what we needed in a mortgage for our new home. Communication was prompt and efficient, everything completed on schedule and we received a lovely welcome pack once we moved in! Great service!

reviews.title: Good service and competitive rates
reviews.text: I have to confess I didn't look too hard for a mortgage. I was happy with the rate I found for the newcastle building society and the service has always been great...so quite happy.

reviews.title: Very easy to use and good interest
reviews.text: Simple, straightforward and no issues.

reviews.title: Easy to open and great account
reviews.text: The online journey was simple and the account will help save for a house deposit

reviews.title: Wish I Had Not Switched
reviews.text: I chose to switch my LISA from a stocks and shares to cash and as Newcastle had the best interest rate at the time I switched to them. I now massively regret not reading the reviews. It took 2 months to transfer the money between the accounts and this meant I nearly missed the deadline for receiving my bonus. I also never received details for online banking and had to go through a painful process of letters and calls to get access. I am now in the process of drawing the money out for the purchase of my home and hope that I have a smoother experience then when I put it in. If doing again I would avoid Newcastle at the expense of the extra 0.2% interest I would earn.

reviews.title: Incompetent
reviews.text: Applied online and told to either fund account before it was open or wait till postal confirmation saying it’s open. No confirmation received months after so thanking my lucky stars I did not send my money into a black hole!! Off to find a company that can actually open an account...

reviews.title: Poor customer and extremely slow service
reviews.text: I applied for mortgage with Newcastle. The mortgage was agreed in principle and valuation was done on 2 April 2019 and I am still waiting for my mortgage offer. What a disgrace. I am sorry I applied and thoroughly disgusted with the pace. I am ashamed to be associated with Newcastle.

reviews.title: Long-term saving
reviews.text: Easy to open, great customer service and my idea of a really great offer.

reviews.title: Great telephone service
reviews.text: I was locked out of my online account (apparently because it had become inactive...I hadn't logged in for a while). But calling them up this morning proved to be very easy. No wait time and very friendly service!

reviews.title: This was a great product...
reviews.text: Logged in the other night to find that this account is no more. Instead of a good interest rate product, I've been moved to a sub <1% savings account. Not great. Looking for somewhere else to move my money too now!

reviews.title: Great face to face customer service
reviews.text: I was informed that Newcastle Building Society would no longer be accepting standing orders, so I tried to set-up a different instruction. Unfortunately something went wrong and a subsequent payment I made to them was rejected. However, when I went into the Linthorpe Road branch, J Roberts was very helpful and dealt with my query quickly...still not sure why the payment was rejected though!

reviews.title: Good interest rate and friendly telephone staff
reviews.text: I opened an account with them because the rate they were offering was one of the best around. Forgot my password a few times, and locked my account...but staff on the phone were really helpful! Recommended

reviews.title: No compassion or understanding for members
reviews.text: I have a lot of money in a 5 year bond taken out when interest rates were very low. I politely asked if I could renegotiate the interest rate by a couple of percentage points but was told no. Whilst I understand that I had a contract I would have liked them to demonstrate some compassion, especially in view of how much their CEO and others are earning.

reviews.title: Great customer service
reviews.text: The staff in my local branch are always so friendly!

reviews.title: 5 out of 5 stars...from me
reviews.text: Ray Sisterson 49 reviews GB Rated 5 out of 5 stars 2nd to none I have been helped today i can not tell you how far this person went to see me right today 100 % Fantastic could not ask for any better so thank you Laura Moorhead 5* from me Date of experience: 15 April 2025

reviews.title: Avoid at all costs
reviews.text: Accused my wife of money laundering when she tried to transfer an ISA to them. Other companies do online checks within seconds but these idiots are incapable of that. Customer Services can only repeat 'Computer says no'. Absolutely useless company.i

reviews.title: No longer a market leader
reviews.text: I joined the Newcastle BS when their "Big Home Saver" regular savings account was a market leader, paying about 3% . Over the last few years the rate has gradually declined to the point where I abandoned the society to pursue better rates elsewhere. Service with Newcastle was always good in my experience, although the online banking service was a little clunky. Overall a B+ (could do better!)

reviews.title: Nice idea but rates still low
reviews.text: I think that this account is a clever thing for the Newcastle Building Society to do - in that it gives a little back to charity. However, for every £1000 in this account, the charity gets just £1 per year and the customer £10. At a 1.1% rate this is low, but top marks on the intention.

reviews.title: TOONARMY
reviews.text: Do a great job for our local hero and legend wot sir Bobby Robson

reviews.title: An amazing service provided!
reviews.text: Newcastle Building Society provides some of the best service I have ever regularly received on the high street. Their attention to communication is excellent. Service isn't limited to requests, they always go the extra mile to find out whether more help is required and assuring my products are suitable and long term financial well being considered.

reviews.title: The Best
reviews.text: put 10,000 in 3% for 5 years brilliant

reviews.title: Frriendly, Efficient Service
reviews.text: Local offices with friendly staff who get to know you and your financial needs. Good old fashioned service from a modern, forward thinking financial institution which still has customers interests at the heart of it.

reviews.title: Helped and well guided throughout
reviews.text: My review is about the personal touch the newcastle provided when I was securing a mortgage. Would have no difficulty to recommend to a friend or family member.

reviews.title: Isa Enquiry
reviews.text: The service/advice provider was dressed immaculately, friendly, informative & met all of my requirements promptly.

reviews.title: Best Rates Only for Selected Customers
reviews.text:
I’ve been a customer of Newcastle BS for couple of years and previously found their rates and service quite good.

However, it’s disappointing to see that some of their best rates are now only being offered to selected customers i.e. those in local areas. 

As an existing customer, it doesn’t feel fair that better deals are not available to everyone. This approach makes loyal customers feel overlooked. 

07.03.2026
Re: More details

In 2026, there’s no reason why offers like 
6 Month Variable Saver (Issue 4) 4.15%, should be restricted to physical branches only. With all the advancements in technology and digital banking, its outdated to expect customers to travel long distances just to open an account in order to get a good deal. 

Offering these deals online would not only be more convenient, but also more inclusive, allowing all customers, regardless of location, to benefit equally from the best rates.

I’d like to see Newcastle BS reconsider this approach and make their best offers available to all customers, online and in branch. It would go a long way in showing that they truly value all their customers, not just those in certain areas. Thank you.

reviews.title: The staff are very polite and friendly…
reviews.text: The staff are very polite and friendly and dealt with my requests very efficiently. I would recommend the Knaresbrough branch to anyone wanting a Building Society account.

reviews.title: Terrible building society
reviews.text: Terrible building society - avoid at all costs

reviews.title: Awful customer service
reviews.text: Trying to communicate with them regarding my elderly father's LPA is a nightmare. Cannot speak to anyone, every email I send gets a reply 4 days later! They sent out paper forms with those tiny character boxes that need posting back - in 2026 hello? Completed the form and posted with the accurate address and...lost!? Aagh!

reviews.title: Brilliant building society

reviews.title: Fantastic service lovely staff they…
reviews.text: Fantastic service lovely staff they went above and beyond to help me. Thank you so much xx

reviews.title: Great Savings Service
reviews.text: Recently opened a Lifetime ISA with Newcastle after a bad experience at Skipton and found the process easy, intuitive and straightforward. Mobile app activated and seems easy to use, deposits accepted quickly and service over the phone has been friendly and knowledgeable. I’d highly recommend Newcastle so far.

reviews.title: Excellent customer service
reviews.text: I want to personally thank Helen Slaughter  and Sophie Allison  both from the Newcastle Building Society  for their exceptional good conduct and indebt knowledge of the company product,  they both deserve a promotion,  they will help you understand and navigate the process of obtaining a mortgage with smiles, they have positive attitude and are never upset when you ask many questions or ask them to repeat something for clearer understanding,  guys ask for this two great women whenever you call Newcastle Building Society  they are the best of the best.

reviews.title: I'm not happy
reviews.text:
You can open an account online but don't walk into Gosforth branch & expect it to happen without an appointment. Even though, the 2 customer service faced staff, had no customers in sight.
Could've been done in a couple of minutes & after all, it's 2025.& not the dark ages.
Their no can do attitude has left me wondering, where should I invest next

reviews.title: Newcastle Building Society – A £1.75 Comedy Showu ever want to experience what…
reviews.text:
If you ever want to experience what banking would've felt like in 1066, look no further than Newcastle Building Society.

All of this chaos — and I mean full-blown sitcom-level chaos — happened over £1.75.

I asked them to reconcile an address issue on my account. They treated this request like I’d asked them to launch a rocket from Gosforth to the moon.
Not only did nothing get fixed, but they somehow made the problem more confusing, which I didn’t think was physically possible.

I requested a simple statement to clarify my £1.75 situation. Apparently that was too much.
Even when I warned them that the next step could involve the finacial ombudsaman, they still couldn’t be bothered. I think the computers went for a lie-down.

Their complaints department? Imagine a medieval castle surrounded by a moat, except instead of archers, it’s staffed by people whose only skill is selective reading.
Letters to the Chief Executive must travel through some sort of complaint-deflecting forcefield because the important points never make it to the other side.

And the best part?
Someone once told me years ago:

"Don’t waste your time with Newcastle Building Society. Their technology is from the Dark Ages and their customer service is worse."

I thought that was an exaggeration.
No. I can now confirm it’s a factual documentary.

So yes — after weeks of hassle, confusion, and technological time travel, all over £1.75, I can proudly say:

This is the most expensive £1.75 laugh I’ve ever had.

reviews.text: I am absolutely fuming at their incompetence. I opened an ISA with them in 2022, as confirmed by emails from them at the time. I have not gone to transfer my ISA to another provider and they have told me it is not an ISA. I've lost all the tax advantages from the last few years, and will be behind on the money in my ISA now for the rest of my life.

reviews.title: Payments into Savings Accounts
reviews.text:
Payments into Savings Accounts
Before opening a savings account with Newcastle Building Society, I would strongly recommend you ask whether there are any restrictions on how you pay into the account ie by cash, cheque or debit card. I am currently in dispute with the Society over cash payments I have made into their Six Monthly Account. They need to be more upfront with customers at the point of opening accounts to avoid potential injury to feelings. I am currently raising this with the Financial Services Ombudsman
A word to the wise: As expected, no-one from Newcastle Building Society (alias Beth) ever got back to me. Beware of false promises!

reviews.title: My late father was introduced to the…
reviews.text: My late father was introduced to the Will Writing Company by this dreadful organisation. A working class man sold down the river to spivs offering unregulated services. It took two years and £4000 for me to sort out the mess that they got him into - no help, no support and certainly nothing to see here - not our fault. Shame on them. My father foolishly trusted them - I have utter contempt for this company.

reviews.title: Great service

When banks have closed in the villages, it is wonderful that a member-owned (mutual) building society is so strongly community-minded... demonstrating the ethos, and providing the  highest standard of service to customers. Thank you

reviews.text: Resent remortgage with Newcastle Building Society, I dealt with Richard who was fantastic! I was traveling for 3 weeks with work and Richard adjusted his hours of work and we held the meeting on Teams. He really couldn’t have been more helpful.

reviews.title: Time to move on I think...
reviews.text: Received an email on Monday inviting me to open a loyalty saver account. I click on the link to open the account, and receive the message..."We cannot direct you to the requested web page at this time. Please try again". Email Customer services and told to wait 24 hours for the IT Department to activate. NOTHING, same error. What a complete waste of my time. So in September, I'll be moving on to another Building Society who can be bothered and transfer my savings to them instead.

reviews.text: I have wasted hours simply trying to revive an account they made dormant.  Same names at same address - should be easy enough.  The hoops they made me jump through are ridiculous.  I took driving license into their Carlisle branch and they wrote to me saying they photocopy was unclear.  THEIR OWN PHOTO COPY DONE BY THEIR OWN STAFF.  Several other stupid mistakes.  They are very hard work.

reviews.title: First rate experience at the Consett branch
reviews.text:
Have been with Newcastle Building Society for a number of years and cannot speak too highly of the staff at the Consett branch.
My business with them today was dealt with the usual friendliness and efficiency even though I had omitted to book an appointment.
In these days of bank closures it is reassuring  to be able to have face to face discussions with friendly people who are genuinely interested in your financial needs.

reviews.title: Highly Recommended.
reviews.text: Always friendly, helpful and professional.  Time is taken with each customer to provide the appropriate servive.  Overall, an excellent service.

reviews.title: Patronising official
reviews.text: I received my first account information letter from Newcastle B/S; it contained an account number and a reference number. I became a bit confused about which was which and went to my local branch to enquire. I was greeted adequately, and when I asked if I could sit down, I received a curt yes. The official came over to me, standing above me like a praying mantis, and I explained the issue I was facing. I told her that the account number and the reference number were confusing for me. To cut a long story short, she spoke to me as if I were five years old; she was so patronising that the following day, I cancelled my account and returned the money to the original bank.

This was really appreciated, and they transferred the ISA the day after it matured. Thank you

Just had one of the best online support experiences from an extraordinarily patient professional called Mary.
I am awful at online anything and have been avoiding the rigamarole of purchasing a new ISA, having not properly set up my online account when instructed to do so by mail. Mary very patiently took me through the process, at all times being good humoured and supportive, and got my online account properly set up and helped me with the purchasing of my ISA. Faultless. Brilliant support. Thanks

My husband had a default on his credit report from years ago when he was at sea on a submarine he went to the company who removed the default as it wasn’t his fault but there’s we provided all this information to Newcastle who only offered us a 80% mortgage even though we have a 90% with them now. Said they wouldn’t consider for another 90 days just forcing us to pay £13000 it’s a scam and would not recommend at all. SCAM

reviews.title: I would like to say thank you very much…
reviews.text: I would like to say thank you very much to Alex Baker for helping us this morning, he was very friendly and very patient. I would just like to let him know that we managed to do the transfer of funds to another account. He helped me to set this up. 5 stars to him. From 2 young pensioners.

reviews.title: excellent customer service
reviews.text: my phone calls were promptly answered and each call handler was friendly, helpful, polite and patient. i was talked through the opening of an online account and difficulties were kindly explained which resulted in the successful opening of an account. reassuring to have someone at the end of the line who has patience with a senior not au fait with online hurdles. 'old fashion' service at its best.  One negative I should mention is the unhelpful paper application form. The only option for making an initial deposit to open an account was a transfer from an existing Newcastle account! or a cheque. Last time I used a cheque was 2018! and the questions were ambiguous so it was difficult to know how to answer them. e.g. how much do you wish to deposit  (which you state) then further down page only option was a cheque or in house transfer as they do not accept a transfer from another building society where funds would come from.  Those funds had to be sent to my bank, then onward to Newcastle so only option was a nominal sum in the form of a cheque to open an account, then the full investment amount could be paid in directly from my bank!

reviews.title: Excellent service
reviews.text: The staff in Whitley Bay branch are  always friendly, helpful and very efficient. No queue, so I was attended to immediately, they always make me feel welcome which is such a change these days. Also it was nice to get a reply to my review a few days later.

reviews.title: Great people
reviews.text: Great people, professional and friendly. Good financial products.  Welcoming atmosphere. Customers feel valued.

reviews.title: Incompetent is being generous to them
reviews.text: Me and my fiancée are currently in the process of buying a house and made the unfortunate mistake of opening LISAs with Newcastle BS. As my fiancées account hadn’t been open for a year the government bonus was going to be clawed back, this was expected, however on completion day they refused to transfer the money to our solicitors, their reasoning it would take them an extra day to transfer due to the bonus being clawed back. Now to be clear Newcastle BS we’re aware of the transfer date 3 weeks ago, yet only decided on completion day that it would take them an extra day to transfer what is legally my fiancées money and not theirs, they can’t seem to explain why it takes them an extra day, and have so far even refused to confirm that the money will now be transferred on the next working day. This was after speaking to a representative who assured my fiancée that she would email her back today, to be clear they did not email. My fiancée said she would like to make a complaint and the representative said ‘what do you hope to achieve from complaining’ that just demonstrates the sheer arrogance and indeed ignorance of this organisation. I appreciate this may not be everyone’s experience, but they really do not take customer service seriously and at this stage are holding onto money that they are not authorised to.

reviews.title: Switching my mortgage to a new deal couldn't go easier
reviews.text: My mortgage deal was due to expire so I contacted the NBS Customer Service and asked for help in terms of switching to a new product that suits our needs. The advisor was very friendly and helpful, she drove us through the products by highlighting pros/cons so we were able to select the best deal for the next term.

reviews.title: Bereavement and Newcastle Building Society
reviews.text: Bereavement causes many problems including financial if accounts are joint but only your partner has generally taken control. With very little knowledge the online staff were able to give help sympathetically to help me take control of my building Society accounts without any problems. My greatest thanks to their team.

reviews.title: Archaic processes and slow service
reviews.text: Held a LISA until Sep 2023 when I switched providers. Recently requested copies of my LISA statements from NBS which I need as part of a house purchase. They are refusing to send me these via email, can’t reinstate access to my online account for me to download them and it’s been more than 2 weeks and I still haven’t received copies in the post. An absolutely archaic way of working, wouldn’t recommend that anybody chooses NBS in the 21st century.

reviews.text: I am very impressed by the very calm, very helpful approach of the two members of staff who are dealing with my banking problem.

reviews.text: Friendly helpful personal service which enabled me to ask questions and clarifications.

reviews.title: Outdated ways of working
reviews.text: We have a mortgage with them on a discounted variable but there is no way of us finding out what the current interest rate is without calling and waiting on hold. Just wish there was an online account you could see an update statement to show how much you owe, monthly payments, current interest rate etc.

reviews.title: Monthly visits
reviews.text: Newcastle building society staff are always pleasant and helpful

reviews.title: Very helpful staff and make you welcome…
reviews.text: Very helpful staff and make you welcome they will go up and above for you 😀

reviews.title: Helpful staff at Darlington branch

reviews.title: PERFECT SERVICE …
reviews.text: PERFECT SERVICE ON MY VISIT TODAY TO NEWCASTLE BUILDING SOCIETY. STAFF WERE VERY HELPFULL AND KNOWLEDGEABLE.

reviews.title: Handcuffed and let down by Newcastle ISA
reviews.text: After saving in a fixed ISA for 5 years and 1 month at approx 2%, the early exit penalty is one year's interest any time before this date, with only 2 days' notice before they drop the money into a very poorly-paying account. Why is Newcastle so out of line with other providers? Beware careful savers - it's all in the small print! Oh, and they tell you it's maturing 14 days before the end of the 61 months but you can't do anything until 48 hours before the end of the 61 months!

reviews.title: Terrible service and tedious bank to…
reviews.text: Terrible service and tedious bank to deal with. If you are able Do Not Use their services. Nothing near helpul or understanding of providing a good customer service.

reviews.title: Top rate service at Northumberland Street Branch
reviews.text: Excellent service by the staff at the Northumberland Street Branch, very efficient hassle free in sorting my requests.

reviews.title: Always  Quality service&in an ideal…
reviews.text: Always  Quality service&in an ideal location.

reviews.title: Superb Cramlington service
reviews.text:
We were helped so much by the girls at Cramlington today.
We had a very sensitive,  safeguarding issue on a relative's account.
Their help totally solved the problem and prevented future difficulties.  Can't speak highly enough about the help we received.  Great knowledge,  understanding, supportive staff.
So grateful.

reviews.title: A pleasant experience on my visit
reviews.text: A pleasant experience on my visit.  Efficient and polite was the Receptionist with no waffle as some companies push onto their customers.

reviews.title: Excellent service
reviews.text: Efficient service and a good product.

reviews.title: Didn't know can use bank debit card
reviews.text: I'm disappointed I won't be able to do a savings account on this occasion with this building society as they are asking too much on how to write the cheque.  I find it impossible to fit all the wording they expect eg Newcastle Building Society, my name and account number on the first line, it's even a terrible squeeze if my name is omitted as an advisor said I could do which I did. Sadly my bank didn't wish to accept the cheque maybe because of my name omitted from the first line.  I wasn't given the option of a debit card payment on the phone, just cheque or bank transfer and now it's too late as done something else but hopefully next time

reviews.title: Excellent staff at Durham Branch
reviews.text:
The friendly professional approach to dealing with older customers.
Richard was especially knowledgeable & helpful explaining & providing all the details needed about the products and efficiently dealing with our transactions.
We were most impressed.

reviews.title: Amazing customer service
reviews.text: Amazing customer service, very helpful. Great service offered. Would recommend to anyone

reviews.title: Very dishonest and cost me thousands of pounds
reviews.text: Very dishonest. prevented me from renewing my mortgage so that they could charge me a higher interest rate which cost me thousands of pounds. Newcastle tried to remove my reviews. I am considering legal action against them as I'm very upset about about how they've treated me.

reviews.title: Very helpful staff
reviews.text: Very helpful staff, offered advice about easy access to my money and kept me informed throughout. West Denton branch.

reviews.title: A local building society that you can…
reviews.text: A local building society that you can deal with face to face and not the internet which All of us don't necessary understand.Any issue go into the office where the staff will help you

reviews.title: They have staff
reviews.text: They have staff, and for me this is  important. There is always a friendly and efficient member of staff to assist.

reviews.title: Very efficient
reviews.text: Very efficient, my account was available to login to in less than a day.  No issues at all.

reviews.title: As a new customer
reviews.text: As a new customer, the application process and accompanying explanations were straightforward from start to finish.

reviews.title: Impossible customer service
reviews.text: Impossible customer service. Wished to discuss my current mortgage product but endless calls to contact centre, get usual garbage "due to unprecedented call volumes, blah blah". Simply not good enough that I cannot get through to my mortgage lender on the phone. Oh, and the form to book an appointment on your website doesn't work...no " prove you are human" entry possible!

reviews.title: Competent
reviews.text: Competent, knowledgeable, helpful and unhurried handling of our financial affairs. What more can you ask for?

reviews.title: Helpful customer service
reviews.text: I was a customer with NBS for years. Found out the account that I had was no longer active as the building society stopped the product service years. Thanks  to Clare, she was able to guide me through the process of closing my account and even explained the LISA account and how it worked. I will definitely be opening another account with NBS.

reviews.title: Friendly helpful professional staff on…
reviews.text: Friendly helpful professional staff on my local high street of Whickham.

reviews.title: Good friendly professional service
reviews.text: Good friendly professional service, easy to deal with.

reviews.title: The importance of face to face customer contact
reviews.text: Nice friendly person served me and sorted out the request I made with effectiveness as a customer I felt valued and MH business imports

reviews.title: They went above and beyond
reviews.text:
I remember going on a customer service course and being taught that you remember companies in how well they deal with things when they go wrong.
Without going into detail, they went above and beyond in dealing with a complaint I raised . I felt they cared, wanted to learn from the experience and dealt with my compliant  in a way I’ve not experienced before. They were really outstanding in dealing with me afterwards and left me feeling very positive towards them .

reviews.title: Painless Transaction
reviews.text: Top class service. Professional and made the transaction painless. Other Societies / Banks should try to emulate this service level to customers as it is first class.

reviews.title: Great fantastic service
reviews.text: Great fantastic service, so easy I had forgotten this sort of service still happened.

reviews.title: I was given time to explain my needs
reviews.text: I was given time to explain my needs. I was able to choose a product and complete the paperwork in a relaxed and supportive environment.

reviews.text: Knowledgeable and helpful staff

reviews.title: Excellent Staff
reviews.text: Knowledgeable friendly staff who give good advice on the products they have...

reviews.title: They are keeping my money against my will
reviews.text:
Avoid at ALL COSTS

So I created a LISA account several years back when I thought to myself I wanted a future in the UK, I now no longer see that future with the state that the country is heading in and so I requested to withdraw my money back on the 26th of March, I'm not capable to give them a call directly so I communicate with them via messages, pretty annoying that every time I sent a message I'd have to wait at least 2 days for a response, so they asked me to confirm that I'm willing to pay the 25% HMRC charge to which I said yes sure, fine, then they asked me to give my account details to which bank I want to withdraw to, so I give them my account details to which account I wanted to withdraw to, then they got back to me telling me that the details I provided with do not match with when I first created the account around half a decade ago... Like hold up a second, this is not what you asked me, you asked to which account I wanted to withdraw my account, so then I respond to them and telling them fine this is the account details of what I opened up with then I got no response, nothing, it is now the 9th of April and still no replies. I send them messages I get ghosted. No replies anymore, at this point they are holding my money against my will and its all because of their miscommunication, you did not ask me what account I used to open up my LISA, you did not ask the security question and now I'm frustrated, I needed that money before the new tax year and here I am, a week later chasing up these scumbags who won't bother to contact me

No I do not wish to communicate with your pathetic email service I want my money out of my account 3 weeks ago, prompto, so don't give me that "I'm sorry to hear that feel free to contact us via so-and-so @ unreliablebank.co.uk"

And if that's what you reply with then that's another reason why your costumer service sucks

Horrendous bank! AVOID AT ALL COSTS

Edit as of the 11th of April : So they have finally managed to sort out my money and withdraw it all, but why did it have to take for me to make a review on here before I finally got someone's attention?

reviews.title: Yarm branch is great...lovely staff and…
reviews.text: Yarm branch is great...lovely staff and friendly service.

reviews.title: Awful company
reviews.text: Awful company, badgered my parents to undertake a trust with a non FCA regulated business, which ended up going bust. NBS washed their hands of the matter “not our fault, nothing to do with us..”. It cost my parents thousands of pounds to get their house back, and could lose their life savings. Disgrace of a company, utterly shameful, benefitting from the most vulnerable to satisfy the greed of their shareholders.

reviews.title: Great experience with the Hawes Branch…
reviews.text: Great experience with the Hawes Branch dedicated staff. Nothing was to much trouble. Will be telling all my friends and family how good the team are they are asset to your company. Very professional.

reviews.title: Excellent customer service
reviews.text: Excellent customer service. Friendly and informative staff.

reviews.title: Poor customer relations…
reviews.text: Applied to open a new account on the 25/03/2024 and received email confirming application saying it would take up to 24hrs. 48hrs later still awaiting confirmation that the account has been opened.

reviews.title: Excellent customer service
reviews.text: Excellent customer service and no long drawn out 'introduction' on the phone - I've been a customer for many years and never have had any cause for complaint; cheerful staff who know what they are talking about - a rare thing these days. Very pleased happy customer.

reviews.title: Quick connection to REAL people
reviews.text: You get quickly connected to REAL competent and friendly people who connect with you in a meaningful way.

reviews.title: Awful company
reviews.text:
Awful company, allegedly ‘connecting you with a better financial future’ well be careful taking any financial advice from them, because it wasn’t the case for my parents. NBS introduced 
my elderly parents, and thousands of others, to a trust with a non FCA regulated business, which ended up going bust. We have letters from NBS introducing my parents to the advisor who recommended my parents transfer their property and life savings into the trust. NBS have since washed their hands of the matter “not our fault, nothing to do with us..”. 
Their website also claims that they have helped communities with their finances for over 150 years. This was not help. The badly managed trust cost my parents thousands of pounds to get their house back, and the loss of their life savings. My elderly mother has suffered shame, anxiety and poor health since realising over 2 years ago. 
NBS are a disgrace, the lack of accountability is utterly shameful, they benefitted from the most vulnerable to satisfy the greed of shareholders. Don’t take their advice or recommendations.

reviews.title: I complained to Newcastle Building…
reviews.text:
I complained to Newcastle Building Society on 11 January about their failure to alert us to their concerns about the Family Trust Corporation - a concern that their "Money Manager" said he advised them to contact us about. They responded on 12 January saying "Thank you for your update and your email yesterday regarding Openwork’s response.
 I’ll investigate the point raised and respond back with my findings." Obviously, because they treat their members with contempt and do not take responsibility for their mistakes, I have heard nothing since.
 
Response from Newcastle BS is to tell me to contact their complaints department - the department that didn't respond to my complaint! I have no intention of wasting my time again so have referred to financial ombudsman (whose sole purpose seems to be to misinterpret complaints in favour of the building societies). The building societies have no governance - they should not be trusted.

reviews.title: We've been a customer of Newcastle BS…
reviews.text: We've been a customer of Newcastle BS for 40 years. As a family we trusted you with our finances completely. We've had to spend thousands of pounds getting our parents home out of the unregulated financial product they were advised to use. Despite our longstanding relationship you have given us no help whatsoever

reviews.title: The Newcastle Building Society casts Pensioners Adrift
reviews.text: The Newcastle building society were responsible me and my wife loosing several thousands of pounds in fees and investments  to an unregistered Trust company they recommended and introduced us to in their Newcastle branch. These Trust companies have now gone bust leaving our homes and money in peril but the Newcastle Building Society have washed their hands of us saying although they received commission for introducing us to the companies they do not accept any responsibility for our losses or situation. This situation affects over 400 pensioners who were introduced to the trust companies by four local Building Societies.

reviews.title: Shame on the Newcastle Building Society…
reviews.text:
Shame on the Newcastle Building Society for introducing and recommending their elderly targeted members to unregulated Companies and products and failing to inform them of this! Then when it all goes wrong, washing their hands of the whole situation. 
The stress this has caused to your clients and their families is immeasurable. Do the right thing Newcastle and accept some responsibility and compensate your clients for their losses.

reviews.title: Do not trust Newcastle Building society…
reviews.text: Do not trust Newcastle Building society with any of your financial affairs. Please warn your elderly family members as this seems to be their target market. Recommendations from Newcastle, Leeds, Nottingham Building societies has resulted in thousands of elderly having their homes at risk and are set to lose multiple thousands of savings. Financial advice from a building society of over 40 years who these clients put their trust in. If you have any doubt, search up Yorkshire Post Phillips trust and building society scandal.

reviews.title: Investing with the Newcastle
reviews.text: We have invested with the Newcastle for many years and needed a new investment as two old ones were maturing. Your interest rates are lower than I'm getting elsewhere which is a pity, but hopefully you will catch up soon and equal other building societies. As the budget was no help to the elderly we look to the building societies to look after us.

reviews.title: Brilliant product and easy online access
reviews.text:
Brilliant product and easy online access. Offset mortgage has helped us save towards final repayment while having instant access to our savings if needed. Linked to Bank of England interest rates it gives us a good return on our investment. 
Thank you and well done!

reviews.title: Professional
reviews.text: Professional, friendly staff. Never been disappointed whilst visiting Newcastle and Chester - le- street branches.

reviews.title: My visit to Newcastle Building society
reviews.text: Staff are always very friendly and helpful and very knowledgeable a mention to Lesley

reviews.title: Great service!
reviews.text: We were advised of a new higher rate account by Susan at Northumberland st branch of Newcastle building society. Susan was helpful efficient, friendly and inspired confidence in new account which was fully explained, we were very happy with service, Susan was a pleasure to speak to throughout. Thanks for all the valued help!

reviews.title: Very friendly and helpful staff and…
reviews.text: Very friendly and helpful staff and very knowledgeable on the building society accounts.

reviews.title: To sign in I've treble-checked every…
reviews.text:
To sign in I've treble-checked every character - letters & numbers - so many times & each time they don't recognise my User ID and my password, sending me endless emails to 'reset' which never provide the opportunity to proceed. 
This has happened previously too!  I hope (!!!) to get through on the telephone tomorrow & transfer all my savings;  I wish I'd read Trust Pilot before I'd applied to join!

reviews.title: Very professional staffing…
reviews.text: Staff very helpful .go out of there way to help.aways give you sound advice

reviews.title: Phoned Gateshead branch to reinvest my…
reviews.text:
Phoned Gateshead branch to reinvest my ISA, the lady I spoke to couldn't get me off the phone quick enough in fact just about hung up on me.
She was no help at all.

reviews.title: What a great website
reviews.text:
What a great website! I have saved with lots of different building societies over the years. 
The Newcastle has by far and away the most navigable and most informative website. It's as if the designer asked themselves what information a customer might be seeking, and then set out to provide it as accessibly as possible. The interest rate was very acceptable too. It's all too easy to write critical reviews, but laudatory ones are important too.

reviews.title: Very helpful, and friendly
reviews.text: Phoned for information and some help. Excellent service, helpful and friendly.

reviews.title: Considerate.
reviews.text: Very helpful on closing and transferring my wifes account on her passing away.

reviews.title: Pleasant member of staff
reviews.text: Pleasant member of staff, clear and very patient. This society excels at the personal touch, lacking in many businesses today.

reviews.title: Very helpful!
reviews.text: the staff contated by telphone were very helpful and sorted out my problem efficiently

reviews.title: Great branch and friendly helpful…
reviews.text: Great branch and friendly helpful colleagues.

reviews.title: Great service at NBS
reviews.text: Great service. The people at Newcastle Building Society were very helpful and polite. Nice cup of tea while I was there.

reviews.title: Good interest rates
reviews.text:
Opened an account without any problems.
Good interest rates

reviews.title: Where is my money?
reviews.text: ISA matured a week ago but yet to be transferred to new institution. I was lied to when they said it had been transferred on the day of maturity because the online account showed it sitting there. My online account is now shut down and I can't access it. Do they deliver my money by hand? I can't think of any other explanation. They cannot compete with the big boys, so it is best to avoid them.

reviews.title: Helpful
reviews.text: Freindly, professional staff who provided the assistance I needed.

reviews.title: Excellent personal service
reviews.text:
So good to be able to deal with money matters in person 
Excellent service and advice

reviews.title: A very pleasant lady helped me all the…
reviews.text: A very pleasant lady helped me all the way through first class service very happy.over 75 years another learner at the internet.

reviews.title: Very neat building society
reviews.text: This is the best building socitey ive ever been too, they were all very nice to me and even bowed down to me ouit of respect. Very Awesome. Better then chocolate chocolate chip ice cream

reviews.title: Dodgy geezer
reviews.text: Asked for help. Got help. But the geezer was a bit rancid and unappealing. (proper pedo vibes). Really ruined the experience. I couldn't take it anymore and had to walk out.

reviews.title: I love the fact this is local
reviews.text: I love the fact this is local. The NBS are committed to keeping branches open which is good news in my eyes.

reviews.title: Avoid
reviews.text: Please do not waste your time with Newcastle Building society. They are in the dark ages when it comes to technology and their customer service is terrible.

reviews.title: Avoid Cash ISA at all costs
reviews.text: Newcastle Building Society took a large deposit of money for a cash ISA. The money was transferred in May. I received a letter today stating the money would be returned and the application wasn't successful due to them not receiving documents? I've had no correspondence via email or post. They had my money for four months and returned with no interest earned on the amount. Four months of no interest. I am disgusted with this appalling service.

reviews.title: Quick and smooth transfer…
reviews.text: My transfer to the Newcastle Building Society went quickly and efficiently. I can't yet comment any further on their service, but pleased so far.

reviews.title: Customer service is good
reviews.text: Customer service is good. But archaic banking systems. Withdrawls are so slow, if you miss cut off it will come next day, and unable on weekends! Mindblowing. Having to switch away for this reason

reviews.title: Instant Access - Charged Penalties For Withdrawal
reviews.text:
I have been trying to close my instant access savings account for 3 weeks now but the Society will not return all my of my capital. I was told I had to wait until the 30th until I could withdraw my balance in full but this is not the case as I am still being charged a penalty. There is no mention of penalties for withdraw in their T&C's nor any mention that you have to wait to withdraw penalty free from what is stated to be an instant access accounts

As an addendum and in response to the comment below from Newcastle, I have already contacted Newcastle on numerous occasions through there secure messaging system as I believe that is the fair route to go down, it is only because of the lack of a clear and honest response that I feel the need to warn others on trustpilot. I would add that I filed a complaint and was told I would get a response within 3 - 5 days - I didn't and I they continue to penalise me for withdrawing funds from an instant access account - it's been over a month now

reviews.title: Good service
reviews.text: I always find the staff at Newcastle Building Society very helpful and friendly I would definitely recommend

reviews.title: Excellent service
reviews.text: I had lost the documentation on a  savings account held with the Building Society through a divorce and two quick changes in address. The young women who dealt with my request for copies of the information held by the Society was polite, efficient, and professional in dealing with my request

reviews.title: Patience of a saint
reviews.text:
Amazingly helpful and patient.
Made a potentially stressful experience really quite enjoyable.

reviews.title: I gave up trying to transfer my isa to…
reviews.text: I gave up trying to transfer my isa to newcastle building society. I was given conflicting instructions by whoever I spoke to and Ive now decided to give up and go elsewhere.

reviews.title: Newcastle building society
reviews.text: Very good experience and very friendly

reviews.title: Great personal service
reviews.text: The staff made me feel comfortable,showed genuine interest in my requirements.

reviews.title: Great help :)
reviews.text: Great help with my savings account today.

reviews.title: Service, we'll received.
reviews.text: The service assistant explained everything to me regarding  my inquiries into a ISA, response to my questions for her were polite and helpful.

reviews.title: Lovely helpful girl who explainedn…
reviews.text: Lovely helpful girl who explained everything she did to bring my savings account up to date and changed it to a better one!

reviews.title: A local building society
reviews.text: A local building society with professional and friendly staff, who explain the ins and outs of investing in a concise manner.

reviews.title: Excellent
reviews.text:
Excellent!  This building society really does use faster payments, and on withdrawal, doesn't hold on to your cash for a day or so whilst you lose interest- as so many do in UK! 
Highly recommended!

reviews.title: Appalling customer service
reviews.text: My sister and I have been trying to register a Lasting Power of Attorney for my mother's account at the Newcastle Building Society for weeks. The phone line waiting times are over half an hour and sometimes one finally gets through only to hear silence. Every person you speak to gives conflicting information. Apparently, the forms sent out for us to complete were missing sections. The NBS claims to have sent a new form and returned her ID documents to my sister on 1st July. She still has not received them. The NBS claims not to have received my form and ID documents, which I posted in their return envelope on 26th June. Absolutely the worst customer service we have received from any company since we have sadly had to take control of many of my mother's affairs due to her dementia. I have no idea where my driving licence is, or whether I will ever get it back, or when we will finally be able to manage my mum's money for her.

reviews.title: NBS problem quickly solved
reviews.text: Had an issue with Newcastle Building Society.  Someone got in touch with me immediately and was able to resolve the matter fairly and promptly.

reviews.title: Impossible to open account, no communication
reviews.text: Still waiting after 3 weeks for an update.  Application for account email received 28/6/23 with ref number, followed instructions to fund account & sent ID documents requested by post despite my details/address not changing in 25+ years, why could this not be verified electronically?  Used 'contact us' link in initial e-mail to request update after a week, no reply.  E-mail sent to complaints.officer@newcastle 12/7/23, another week later & still no reply - so please don't use this standard reply as I've already tried it without success. Did you receive my ID paperwork?  Is it missing & not reached you?  Should I now be concerned for identity theft?  Where is my deposit I sent 28/6/23?  It has not been returned to my bank.

reviews.title: Denial
reviews.text: They referred my complaint to a third party supplier organisation without discussing that with me - the Newcastle BS sales and marketing team were responsible for my mother in laws savings being placed into a trust managed by an unregulated company - that is the simple fact that they deny responsibility for.

reviews.title: UTTER SHAMBLES OF A BSOC
reviews.text: UTTER SHAMBLES OF A BSOC - WORST CUSTOMER SERVICE EVER. IS LOCKING SAVERS IN TO INSTANT ACCESS ACCOUNT

reviews.title: Difficult and error filled joining process
reviews.text: Opened a savings account because of their market leading rate.  The set up process went wrong when the website referred to an authentication code in the joining letter which wasn’t there.  I requested the code twice - each time it took 3 days to arrive and each time I got an error message on the website.  Newcastle BS have a difficult, error filled joining process which needs fixing.  Luckily I only tested a £1 deposit, so I’m off to another company

reviews.title: Untrustworthy building society
reviews.text:
Opened their Easy Access account over 2 weeks ago. Still waiting for the verification code to arrive, therefore I cannot access any of my money.

Stupidly, I put all my savings into this account and am now unable to pay my regular bills as cannot make any withdrawals.

Emails and Telephone calls do not get answered.

Please do not trust Newcastle Building Society will any of your money.

I'll be contacting the Financial Ombudsman

reviews.title: Weary
reviews.text: I've been patiently attempting to open an account with this building society but it's been a bit of a trial. I have several accounts with financials, but I've never experienced such a labourious, slow, convoluted & bothersome process, with a series of letters/e.mails repeating the same information, or giving confusing 1/2 instructions, multiple authentication codes, etc. Whoever designed it didn't do a v. good job with the customer in mind. Most financials make this a very straightforward process, but this one seems quite the opposite. Even after 9 days I'm not sure it's finished! It doesn't give a good impression to new clients.

reviews.title: Please avoid this company
reviews.text: Please avoid this company. We have been completely crippled financially because of an admin error they made and will not rectify. We’re currently having to go through the ombudsman to regain financial security. No doubt like all unscrupulous companies they will reply to this saying “we’ve sorted your issue”. Do not fall for it please.

reviews.title: Like Hotel California you can never leave
reviews.text: Savings account claims to be easy access and although it's fairly easy to add funds it appears impossible to withdraw any. Apparently you need to authenticate your bank account but having waited a week for the code to finally be posted it didn't work so now have to wait another week for another code. Surely there are quicker ways to authenticate your account. Another short sighted financial institution who offered market leading interest rates without the infrastructure to back it up.

reviews.title: So much hassle
reviews.text: So much hassle to put money in. Even worse trying to withdraw your own money. Every other bank account I have transfers funds instantly. Done my first withdrawal after the long process of authorisation. Still waiting for the money in my bank. The money disappears instantly from the Newcastle savings account but just gets lost somewhere and took a day to appear. I think they are holding the money so they make that little extra out of you. A word of warning also looks like you cannot move your money outside of banking hours, as it just sits pending. I am not impressed one bit and would definitely not recommend.

reviews.title: Get this issue sorted if you want to keep customers!!
reviews.text: Like many on here still waiting for my verification code, so that I can link my "Easy Access Account" to withdraw funds! Not so easy hey! Have ordered a further code as have monies I now need to access! Come on guys! It's the 21st Century get this sorted ASAP!!

reviews.title: Long waiting time on phone...
reviews.text: Opened a savings account all went really smoothly apart from needing to call them with a query but took 52 minutes to answer so dropped a star.

reviews.title: Do they know what they are doing?
reviews.text: Like other reviewers opened a savings account with minimum deposit, received two letters confirming the account ID which I received online anyway when opening the account.  Waiting 5 days for the verification code for nominated bank account, which when I entered onto the account it said was an error!!  The code came on their letter - how could I have got it wrong!.  Sent secure message through the account days ago regarding confirmation of the account number for making future deposits and not a sign that it has even been read let alone responded to.  Dont feel my money would be safe here.

reviews.title: HOPELESS
reviews.text: BEEN WAITING NEARLY 2 WEEKS FOR PIN NUMBER TO VERIFY LINKED ACCOUNT SO TRIED CALLING, HOPELESS, TALKING ABOUT 1 HOUR WAIT.

reviews.title: Why is it now that everything and I…
reviews.text:
Why is it now that everything and I mean everything is Crap .....Nothing works ......Deposited money with these clowns a week ago and now I cannot move it out because I need a verification code which they keep telling me is coming in the post ...Have had three letters now telling me that the account is open but I cannot withdraw anything! ...........So I rang them but was told that the queue was one hour long ! .....Keep being told that the call is important to them !...Well pick up the flippin phone then ! 
Wish I hadn't bothered depositing money with this lot ....As the old saying goes if it seems too good to be true it probably is ...Also the 'holding on'  music they are playing sounds mental ..Sounds like a child of five playing  ..Suggest a change of theme music .....How about Hanging on the telephone by Blondie .....That would be more appropriate ! ....

reviews.title: What customer service?
reviews.text: Have opened an easy access account and no authentication code within the welcome letter, only the ID reference which I'd already received during the online application. Spent an hour and a half in the telephone queue and then call terminated on the stroke of 6 p.m. So relieved I've only paid in the minimum £1 opening amount because I've rapidly lost all faith with this company and will be closing the account and taking my business elsewhere.

reviews.title: Customer service is fantastic.
reviews.text: Customer service is fantastic.

reviews.title: Opened a tracker saver
reviews.text: Opened a tracker saver. I changed User i.d. and password and it worked fine. Deposited £2 to test the system. Logged in with new User i.d. and password the next day and password not accepted. Changed password again then accepted. Put my wife down as 2nd holder it now tells me I am the 2nd holder on the account!!!! Cannot test the account by refunding £1 back to my bank because it needs an authorisation letter which they will send!  Held on hold via their number 0345 7344345 option 1 for 30 minutes nobody will answer. Goodbye Newcastle Building Society you have a major problem with your IT system for this product having just been released which in my opinion is at Beta Stage at best.
